source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 16.3.1 journalctl (pp. 316-318)

questions.q: Show the journal for the sshd.service unit only.
questions.why: -u <unit> filters to that unit; you can repeat -u for several units.

questions.q: Show the kernel messages from the current boot.
questions.why: -k shows kernel messages (dmesg-style) from the current boot; -b limits to the current boot generally.
--since (or -S) filters by start time; --until (-U) sets the end time.

questions.q: Show only journal entries of priority err (3) or higher.
questions.why: -p PRIORITY shows that level and above; err equals 3, so -p err == -p 0..3.

options: Shows the log since the start of the current boot
questions.why: -b limits output to the current boot; --list-boots lists recorded boots.

questions.q: Show how much disk space the journal files occupy.
questions.why: --disk-usage reports the space used; --vacuum-size=BYTES then trims it down.

questions.q: Reduce the journal on disk to at most 500M.
questions.why: --vacuum-size deletes old entries until the journal fits the given size (suffixes K/M/G/T); --vacuum-time trims by age.

questions.q: Search the journal for entries matching the pattern 'error' (grep-style).
questions.why: -g / --grep matches log content by pattern (regex); an all-lowercase pattern is case-insensitive by default.
